chief_complaint: A 55-year-old woman presents with relapse of nephrotic syndrome.
history: The patient has a history of breast cancer treated with tamoxifen and was diagnosed with minimal change nephrotic syndrome (MCNS) by renal biopsy. She achieved remission with prednisolone 50 mg/day, but relapsed approximately 12 days after starting sulfamethoxazole-trimethoprim (ST) therapy for Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia prophylaxis.
exam: Generalized erythema and signs of nephrotic syndrome.
labs: Renal biopsy confirmed MCNS, and drug-induced lymphocyte stimulation tests for ST, atorvastatin, and esomeprazole were negative.
correct_diagnosis: Drug-induced relapse of minimal change nephrotic syndrome
differentials: Drug-induced relapse of minimal change nephrotic syndrome
correct_ranking: Drug-induced relapse of minimal change nephrotic syndrome
